RepositoryMetadata.dc:title: Zero Trust Security Architecture — Utility OT/IT
RepositoryMetadata.dc:description: Enterprise-grade Zero Trust Security Architecture for a multi-utility organisation covering IT and OT domains. Aligned to CISA ZTA five pillars: Identity, Devices, Network, Applications, Data, and Visibility. Maps to IEC 62443 security levels, NERC CIP requirements, and NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust principles.
RepositoryMetadata.dc:creator: Enterprise Architecture Office
RepositoryMetadata.dc:subject: Zero Trust · OT Security · IEC 62443 · NERC CIP · Utility

Principle.Name: Never Trust, Always Verify
Principle.Statement: No user, device, or network location is implicitly trusted. Every access request must be authenticated, authorised, and continuously validated regardless of network origin — internal or external.
Principle.Rationale: Traditional perimeter-based security is insufficient against modern threats targeting utility OT/IT environments including nation-state actors and ransomware.
Principle.Implications: All systems must support identity-based access controls. Legacy systems without identity capabilities require compensating controls or replacement.

Principle.Name: Least Privilege Access
Principle.Statement: Users, devices, and applications are granted the minimum level of access required to perform their function. Privileged access is time-limited (JIT) and requires explicit approval workflows.
Principle.Rationale: Minimises blast radius of a compromised credential or device, particularly critical for OT environments where a single compromised account could affect grid operations.
Principle.Implications: PAM solution required for all OT privileged access. Zero standing privileges policy must be enforced. RBAC and ABAC models required across all systems.
Principle.References: NIST SP 800-207 · IEC 62443-3-3 SR 1.3 · NERC CIP-007

Principle.Name: Assume Breach
Principle.Statement: Architecture is designed with the assumption that a breach has already occurred or will occur. Containment, detection, and response capabilities are as important as prevention.
Principle.Rationale: Utility organisations are high-value targets for sophisticated adversaries. Volt Typhoon, SANDWORM, and similar nation-state actors have demonstrated capability to maintain persistent access within OT environments.
Principle.Implications: Micro-segmentation mandatory. Continuous monitoring required across all zones. Incident response playbooks must be OT-specific and regularly exercised.

Principle.Name: Verify Explicitly — Contextual Access Decisions
Principle.Statement: Access decisions must incorporate all available signals: identity, device health, location, time, risk score, and behavioural context. No single factor is sufficient in isolation.
Principle.Rationale: Multi-factor, contextual access decisions significantly reduce the risk of credential theft and account compromise attacks targeting utility control systems.
Principle.Implications: Policy Decision Point (PDP) must aggregate all signals. Conditional access policies required in IAM platform. UEBA required for behavioural baselining.
Principle.References: NIST SP 800-207 · CISA ZTM · IEC 62443-3-3 SR 1.1

Principle.Name: OT-IT Separation with Controlled Conduits
Principle.Statement: OT and IT networks must remain physically and logically separated. All data exchange between zones must traverse controlled conduits with explicit allow-list rules. No direct IT-to-OT initiation is permitted.
Principle.Rationale: OT systems control physical utility infrastructure. Compromise of OT systems can result in physical damage, public safety incidents, and regulatory penalties.
Principle.Implications: Industrial DMZ mandatory between IT and OT. Data diodes required for historian replication paths. No VPN or remote access tool may bridge IT and OT zones directly.

Principle.Name: Data-Centric Security
Principle.Statement: Security controls follow the data, not just the network perimeter. All sensitive data must be classified, encrypted, and access-controlled regardless of location — at rest, in transit, and in use.
Principle.Rationale: Utility data includes NERC CIP-protected BES Cyber System Information, customer PII, and grid topology data — all requiring different protection levels that must persist beyond network boundaries.
Principle.Implications: Data classification framework mandatory. Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3) required. DLP policies must be enforced at all data egress points.
Principle.References: NIST SP 800-207 · NERC CIP-011 · IEC 62351 · GDPR

Requirement.Name: Multi-Factor Authentication — All Remote OT Access
Requirement.Description: MFA (FIDO2 or TOTP minimum) must be enforced for all remote access to OT systems including SCADA, EMS, DMS, and engineering workstations.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: Zero non-MFA remote OT sessions permitted. PAM audit log demonstrates 100% MFA coverage.

Requirement.Name: Privileged Access Management — OT/IT
Requirement.Description: All privileged accounts for OT and IT systems must be managed via PAM solution (CyberArk). Zero standing privileges policy. JIT access with time-bound credentials.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: No shared or embedded credentials. All OT access via PAM vault. Session recording enabled for all privileged sessions.

Requirement.Name: Non-Human Identity — OT Device Certificates
Requirement.Description: All IEDs, RTUs, PLCs, and smart meters must be issued X.509 certificates from the OT PKI. Certificate-based authentication must replace username/password for OT device communications.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: 100% OT field devices enrolled in PKI. Certificate validity monitoring automated. Revocation via CRL/OCSP operational.

Requirement.Name: Single Sign-On — Enterprise IT
Requirement.Description: All IT systems must federate to Azure AD Entra ID for SSO. SAML 2.0 or OIDC integration mandatory for all new applications.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: No local accounts on IT systems (except break-glass). All applications SSO-enabled. Conditional access policies applied.

Requirement.Name: Industrial DMZ — IT/OT Boundary
Requirement.Description: An industrial DMZ (iDMZ) must be implemented between IT (L4) and OT (L3) zones. No direct routing between IT and OT networks. All data exchange via controlled proxy or data diode.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: Penetration test confirms no direct IT→OT path. All flows logged. Data diode deployed for historian replication.

Requirement.Name: Zero Trust Network Access — Remote Access
Requirement.Description: Legacy VPN must be replaced with ZTNA solution (Zscaler or equivalent). All remote access gated on identity + device posture. No network-level access without application-level authorisation.
Requirement.Source: NIST SP 800-207 · CISA ZTA Network Pillar
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: VPN decommissioned. All remote sessions via ZTNA broker. Device compliance check enforced pre-connection.

Requirement.Name: NGFW with ICS DPI — All Zone Boundaries
Requirement.Description: Next-generation firewalls with ICS-aware deep packet inspection must be deployed at every Purdue zone boundary. Default-deny policy. Explicit allow-list per protocol and direction.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: No default-permit rules. ICS DPI enabled for DNP3, Modbus, IEC 61850. Quarterly rule review documented.

Requirement.Name: Micro-Segmentation — OT Cell/Area Zones
Requirement.Description: OT networks must be segmented at cell and area level per IEC 62443 zone model. Each generating unit, substation, or AMI zone must be isolated with its own security boundary.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: Zone diagram maintained and reviewed. Lateral movement between OT cells blocked. VLAN isolation per zone confirmed.

Requirement.Name: Encryption at Rest — All Sensitive Data
Requirement.Description: All NERC CIP-protected data, customer PII, and grid topology data must be encrypted at rest using AES-256. Oracle DB transparent data encryption mandatory for MDMS and SAP schemas.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: TDE enabled on all production databases. Key management via HSM. Encryption verified in annual audit.

Requirement.Name: Encryption in Transit — TLS 1.3 Minimum
Requirement.Description: All IT network communications must use TLS 1.3 minimum. TLS 1.0 and 1.1 must be deprecated. OT protocol security extensions (IEC 62351, OPC-UA Security) must be enabled.
Requirement.Source: NIST SP 800-52 Rev2 · IEC 62351 · OPC-UA Security
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: TLS scan confirms no TLS 1.0/1.1 in use. OPC-UA sign+encrypt mode enabled on all L2↔L3 sessions.

Requirement.Name: Data Classification and DLP
Requirement.Description: All data assets must be classified per sensitivity: Public, Internal, Confidential, BES Cyber System Information (BCSI). DLP policies must prevent unauthorised exfiltration of BCSI and PII.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: Classification labels applied to all data stores. DLP policy coverage confirmed. Monthly DLP incident report reviewed by CISO.

Requirement.Name: Unified SIEM — IT and OT Log Sources
Requirement.Description: A unified SIEM (Splunk) must ingest logs from all IT and OT systems. OT log collection must be passive and non-intrusive. Minimum 13-month log retention for NERC CIP compliance.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: All Purdue zones represented in SIEM. Log gap analysis monthly. 13-month retention confirmed. NERC CIP evidence extraction automated.

Requirement.Name: OT Threat Detection Platform
Requirement.Description: A dedicated OT threat detection platform (Dragos or Claroty) must be deployed for passive monitoring of OT networks. ICS-specific threat signatures and protocol behavioural analysis required.
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: OT platform deployed in all OT zones. Asset inventory auto-populated. ICS-specific detections tuned. OT SOC 24x7 coverage.

Requirement.Name: UEBA — User and Entity Behaviour Analytics
Requirement.Description: UEBA capability (Microsoft Sentinel) must baseline normal behaviour for all privileged users and OT service accounts. Anomalous behaviour must generate alerts within 15 minutes.
Requirement.Source: NIST SP 800-207 · CISA ZTA Analytics Pillar
Requirement.AcceptanceCriteria: Baseline established for all privileged accounts. Anomaly detection rate <5% false positives. Mean time to detect <15 minutes.

BuildingBlock.Name: Enterprise Identity Provider (IdP)
BuildingBlock.Description: Centralised identity service providing authentication, authorisation, and SSO for all IT systems and OT remote access pathways.

BuildingBlock.Name: Privileged Access Management (PAM)
BuildingBlock.Description: Vault-based PAM solution managing all privileged credentials for IT and OT systems. Enforces JIT access, session recording, and zero standing privileges policy.
BuildingBlock.Protocols: SSH · RDP · PAM REST API · LDAP

BuildingBlock.Name: OT Public Key Infrastructure (PKI)
BuildingBlock.Description: Dedicated PKI for OT domain providing X.509 certificates to IEDs, RTUs, PLCs, smart meters, and OT service accounts. Physically separated from enterprise PKI.

BuildingBlock.Name: OT Asset Discovery and Inventory
BuildingBlock.Description: Passive, non-intrusive OT asset discovery using network traffic analysis. Builds and maintains authoritative OT asset register including firmware versions and vulnerability status.
BuildingBlock.Protocols: Passive SPAN · DPI · PROFINET · DNP3 · IEC 61850

BuildingBlock.Name: Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR/XDR)
BuildingBlock.Description: IT endpoint detection and response. OT endpoints use passive-only Claroty monitoring to avoid operational impact on real-time control systems.

BuildingBlock.Name: Next-Generation Firewall with ICS DPI
BuildingBlock.Description: NGFW deployed at all Purdue zone boundaries. ICS-aware DPI inspects DNP3, Modbus, IEC 61850, and OPC-UA traffic. Default-deny policy with explicit protocol allow-lists per zone conduit.
BuildingBlock.Technology: Palo Alto Networks PA-Series / Fortinet FortiGate
BuildingBlock.Protocols: TCP/IP · TLS · DNP3 · Modbus · IEC 61850 · OPC-UA

BuildingBlock.Name: Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA)
BuildingBlock.Description: Cloud-delivered ZTNA replacing legacy VPN. Evaluates identity, device posture, and context before granting application-level access. No implicit network access granted.

BuildingBlock.Name: Hardware Data Diode — Unidirectional Gateway
BuildingBlock.Description: Hardware-enforced one-way data transfer from OT historian to IT data lake. Physics-based security — no TCP/IP return path possible. Used for PI historian replication and event streaming.

BuildingBlock.Name: Industrial Demilitarised Zone (iDMZ)
BuildingBlock.Description: Dual-firewall iDMZ between IT (L4) and OT (L3) zones. Hosts data diode, Kafka relay, jump server, SIEM collector, OT PKI, and patch proxy. No direct routing between IT and OT.
BuildingBlock.Technology: Dual Palo Alto NGFW · Kafka · CyberArk Jump Server

BuildingBlock.Name: API Gateway with mTLS
BuildingBlock.Description: Managed API gateway enforcing mutual TLS, OAuth2 scoped tokens, and rate limiting for all application-to-application integrations. All inter-system calls must traverse API gateway — no point-to-point.

BuildingBlock.Name: OPC-UA Secure Channel
BuildingBlock.Description: OPC-UA security mode Sign+Encrypt enforced for all L2↔L3 communications. Certificate-based mutual authentication between SCADA server, HMI, DCS, and engineering workstations.

BuildingBlock.Name: Secrets Management Platform
BuildingBlock.Description: Dynamic secrets management for application credentials, API keys, and database passwords. Auto-rotating credentials eliminate long-lived secrets. Kubernetes and CI/CD pipeline integration.
BuildingBlock.Protocols: Vault API · mTLS · LDAP · Kubernetes SA

BuildingBlock.Name: Data Classification and DLP
BuildingBlock.Description: Automated data classification applying sensitivity labels to all data assets. DLP policies prevent unauthorised exfiltration of BCSI, PII, and grid topology data at all egress points.
BuildingBlock.Protocols: MIP Labels · DLP Policy Engine · REST API

BuildingBlock.Name: Hardware Security Module (HSM) and Key Management
BuildingBlock.Description: FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSMs providing hardware root of trust for all cryptographic operations. Centralised key management lifecycle for enterprise and OT PKI, database encryption, and TLS certificates.

BuildingBlock.Name: Security Information and Event Management (SIEM)
BuildingBlock.Description: Unified SIEM ingesting IT and OT log sources. Provides correlation rules, automated alerting, NERC CIP evidence collection, and feeds SOAR for automated response.
BuildingBlock.Protocols: Splunk HEC · Syslog · REST API · Universal Forwarder

BuildingBlock.Name: OT Threat Detection Platform
BuildingBlock.Description: Dedicated ICS threat detection using passive network monitoring. Provides OT-specific threat intelligence, protocol behavioural analysis, asset inventory, and integration to SIEM/SOAR.
BuildingBlock.Technology: Dragos Platform / Claroty Continuous Threat Detection
BuildingBlock.Protocols: Passive SPAN · DNP3 · IEC 61850 · Modbus · PROFINET

BuildingBlock.Name: User and Entity Behaviour Analytics (UEBA)
BuildingBlock.Description: ML-based behaviour analytics baselining normal patterns for users, service accounts, and OT entities. Detects lateral movement, impossible travel, privilege escalation, and anomalous OT commands.
BuildingBlock.Protocols: REST API · KQL · Azure Monitor · CEF

SolutionBuildingBlock.Name: Azure AD Entra ID — Production Tenant
SolutionBuildingBlock.Product: Azure Active Directory / Microsoft Entra ID P2

SolutionBuildingBlock.Name: Splunk Enterprise Security — Production
SolutionBuildingBlock.DeploymentModel: On-premises cluster (IT+OT data separation by index)

SolutionBuildingBlock.Name: Dragos Platform — OT SOC
SolutionBuildingBlock.DeploymentModel: On-premises — passive sensors in all OT zones

Decision.Name: ZTNA to Replace VPN for All OT Remote Access
Decision.Issue: Legacy VPN provides implicit network access and cannot enforce device posture checks, creating unacceptable risk for OT remote access.
Decision.Decision: Deploy Zscaler Private Access as ZTNA solution. Legacy VPN to be decommissioned by Q4 2025. All OT vendor remote access migrated to ZTNA + PAM JIT model.

Alternatives.Alternative: Upgrade VPN with additional NAC — Rejected: Does not eliminate implicit network trust
Software-defined perimeter (SDP) — Rejected: Vendor maturity concerns for OT use cases

Decision.Consequences: VPN infrastructure decommission cost saving. Increased visibility of all remote sessions. Vendor access process change required.

Decision.Name: Hardware Data Diode for All Historian Replication
Decision.Issue: Software-based data transfer solutions between OT historian and IT data lake present attack surface — TCP/IP return path can be exploited.
Decision.Decision: Deploy Waterfall hardware data diode for all OSIsoft PI historian replication from OT (L3) to IT (L4). Software-based replication tools decommissioned.

Alternatives.Alternative: Software proxy with strict firewall rules — Rejected: TCP return path remains a risk vector
Manual data transfer (USB) — Rejected: Operationally impractical and introduces physical media risks

Decision.Consequences: One-way replication only — no writeback from IT to OT historian. Additional latency of 30–60 seconds acceptable for analytics use cases.

Decision.Name: Dedicated OT PKI Separate from Enterprise PKI
Decision.Issue: Sharing enterprise PKI for OT device certificates creates dependency on IT availability and increases blast radius if enterprise PKI is compromised.
Decision.Decision: Deploy dedicated OT PKI with offline root CA and HSM-backed issuing CA. OT PKI physically isolated in OT network. Enterprise PKI continues for IT systems only.

Alternatives.Alternative: Shared enterprise PKI with dedicated OT issuing CA — Rejected: Shared root CA creates unacceptable cross-domain risk

Decision.Consequences: Separate PKI operational team and processes required. Additional HSM hardware cost. Improved OT resilience and isolation.
Decision.ApprovedBy: Architecture Review Board · CISO · OT Manager

MaturityAssessment.framework: CISA Zero Trust Maturity Model v2

KeyInitiatives.Initiative: MFA enforced — all users and remote access (Q2 2025)
PAM JIT access model — all OT systems (Q3 2025)
X.509 device certificates — all IEDs and RTUs (Q4 2025)
Continuous access evaluation via Entra ID CAE (Q1 2026)
OT asset discovery — Claroty passive deployment (Q2 2025)
IT EDR (Defender) — 100% endpoint coverage (Q2 2025)
Device compliance gating for ZTNA access (Q3 2025)
Vulnerability management programme — OT patching (Q4 2025)
iDMZ deployment — IT/OT boundary (Q1 2025)
ZTNA replacing VPN — all remote access (Q4 2025)
Data diode — historian replication (Q2 2025)
OT micro-segmentation — cell/area level (Q2 2026)
API gateway — all inter-system integration (Q3 2025)
OPC-UA sign+encrypt — L2/L3 communications (Q4 2025)
Secrets management — HashiCorp Vault (Q2 2026)
Application threat modelling programme (Q3 2026)
Data classification — all BCSI and PII assets (Q3 2025)
Database TDE — Oracle MDMS and SAP (Q4 2025)
DLP policies — all data egress points (Q1 2026)
Data access governance — Varonis deployment (Q2 2026)
Splunk SIEM — IT+OT unified log ingestion (Q2 2025)
Dragos OT threat detection — all OT zones (Q3 2025)
SOAR playbooks — OT incident response (Q4 2025)
UEBA — Sentinel ML anomaly detection (Q1 2026)
Continuous compliance monitoring — NERC CIP automated (Q1 2026)

Map.description: All data sources treated as resources
All communications secured regardless of location

Gap.Name: OT Device Certificate Coverage — Legacy IEDs
Gap.Description: Approximately 35% of deployed IEDs are pre-IEC 61850 Edition 2 and cannot support X.509 certificate authentication. Compensating controls required until device replacement programme completes.
Gap.CompensatingControl: Physical port locking, VLAN isolation, enhanced monitoring via Dragos passive DPI

Gap.Name: OT Patch Management — Unpatched Legacy Controllers
Gap.Description: Multiple PLCs and RTUs running end-of-support firmware with known CVEs. Vendor patch testing cycles extend to 12–18 months for critical control systems.
Gap.CompensatingControl: Network segmentation, Dragos monitoring, change freeze on affected systems
Gap.Resolution: Accelerated vendor patch qualification programme — ongoing

Gap.Name: OT Micro-Segmentation — Cell/Area Level
Gap.Description: Current OT network segmentation is at zone level (L1/L2/L3). Cell and area level micro-segmentation required per IEC 62443-3-2 but not yet implemented.
Gap.CompensatingControl: Enhanced monitoring and strict zone-level firewall rules

Gap.Name: Third-Party Vendor Access — OT Systems
Gap.Description: Some OT vendors still using legacy VPN or direct modem connections for remote maintenance. Transition to ZTNA + PAM JIT model requires vendor contractual and technical changes.
Gap.CompensatingControl: Enhanced monitoring of vendor sessions, time-restricted access windows
Gap.Resolution: Vendor access policy update and ZTNA onboarding — Q3 2025
